original_jd_summary: A 12-month Graduate Program for Software Engineering candidates to design, develop, test, and evaluate IT software within an agile global leadership environment, focusing on mission-critical IT services and application modernization.

company_context.mission_values: DXC operates with a 'people first' mindset and a 'virtual first' operational model. They are committed to radical inclusion, diversity (neurodiversity, LGBTQIA+, Indigenous communities), and delivering innovation that 'moves the world'.
company_context.culture: Ranked #23 in Best Global Company Culture, DXC offers a collaborative, team-centered environment. It emphasizes mentorship through a dedicated 'people manager' and 'buddy' system from day one, valuing potential over pedigree.
recent_news: Ranked #23 for Best Global Company Culture by Comparably (2025/2026).
Introduced AMBER, a next-generation automotive software platform, at CES 2026.
Achieved 90%+ productivity gains through the successful integration of neurodivergent talent.
company_context.strategic_direction: Focusing on modernization, security, and AI-driven outcomes while maintaining a virtual-first model. Heavy investment in internal training and cloud certifications (Azure/AWS) to handle exponential technological change.

role_details.overview: As a Graduate Software Engineer, you will contribute to the design and evaluation of IT software within a structured 12-month journey. The role focuses on application modernization and leveraging AI to drive customer success.
key_responsibilities: Design, develop, test, and evaluate IT software components.
Collaborate with senior leaders and cross-functional teams in a virtual-first environment.
Participate in ongoing training and professional development to clear industry certifications (e.g., Azure Fundamentals).
Modernize and secure complex technology estates for global enterprise clients.
role_details.success_metrics: Attainment of primary cloud certifications within the first year, successful graduation from the 12-month program, and behavioral alignment with DXC's collaborative and curious mindset.

required_qualifications: Completion of degree requirements between November 2024 and February 2027.
Strong foundational knowledge in software design, development, and testing methodologies.
preferred_qualifications: Interest in Cloud technologies (Azure/AWS) and Artificial Intelligence.
Experience or strong interest in Application Modernization and Managed Infrastructure.
Active engagement in continuous learning and professional certification pathways.
cultural_fit_attributes: Adaptability to a virtual-first working model.
Curiosity and a drive for continuous professional development.
Openness to diverse perspectives and neurodivergent working styles.

company_strengths: Global leader in Managed Infrastructure and Application Modernization.
Award-winning corporate culture and commitment to workplace flexibility.
Deeply structured mentorship and 'buddy' support systems.
what_you_get.growth_opportunities: Structured 12-month journey with clear pathways to Cloud and AI certifications, plus potential for early-career recognition through internal awards (Amethyst, Emerald, Ruby).
challenges_to_tackle: Navigating complex technology estates during AI-driven transformations.
Collaborating effectively across multiple geographies in a fully virtual-first workforce.

interview_preparation.likely_focus_areas: Evaluation of 'technical potential' alongside behavioral alignment with the 'people-first' culture. Anticipate questions on software lifecycle (design/test) and virtual collaboration.
key_themes: Problem Solving in Complex IT Environments
